title: check-anchors.sh allows duplicate anchor IDs when the duplicate pairs remain balanced
evidence: A temp-packet probe that inserted a second balanced metadata anchor still passed ANCHORS_VALID because the rule only compares open/close counts and required-anchor order. The stricter duplicate-ID guard exists in preflight.ts, but not in packet validation.
recommendation: Teach ANCHORS_VALID to reject duplicate IDs directly, or explicitly document that duplicate-anchor enforcement only happens in preflight so packet validation is not treated as authoritative on anchor uniqueness.

subject: p1-or-p0-escalation
reason: The duplicate-anchor blind spot is real, but this pass did not demonstrate packet corruption, data loss, or a release-blocking runtime failure; preflight still provides a partial guard on duplicate IDs.
